key_points: Roommate conflicts are real; consider single rooms seriously
Theft reported in locker rooms; $1300 coat stolen
Dorm communal kitchens trashed regularly; custodians overwhelmed
key_quotes: IF YOU ARE ON THE FENCE BETWEEN A SINGLE AND A DOUBLE (w/ a random) GET A SINGLE
To the guy who stole my $1300 coat out of the Noyes locker room: Why man. It's literally 10°
Third floor at cascadilla has been awful with the trash in the community kitchen all year

key_points: Social scene feels fake and elite to many students
Loneliness and exclusion are recurring freshman complaints
Greek life exists but cliques are noted as a real problem
key_quotes: Everyone I've encountered on campus is so fake and so awful and so elite and so out of touch
I put myself out there and thought I found my group. Weeks into the semester...they left me out
I rushed a sorority (I'm even living in the house), and tried to put myself out there as much as possible

key_points: Dining hall is visually stunning, praised for atmosphere
Cornell Dairy Bar ice cream is a beloved campus institution
Food quality has reportedly declined over the decades per alumni
key_quotes: Your dining hall is gorgeous! It looks like something out of the Harry Potter series
I fell in love with Cornell as I was eating their ice cream at the Dairy Bar after the tour
More administration. Lower quality food. Smaller trays. Drastically changed

key_points: Academic pressure is intense; imposter syndrome is widespread
Loneliness and depression complaints appear frequently from freshmen
Students are explicitly warned about suicide risk near gorges
key_quotes: I am so miserable. I hated it here last year and everyone told me it was just a freshman year thing
These past four years have absolutely destroyed me
It still hurts to see people you considered friends leave you out or exclude you from pictures

key_points: Cornell is need-blind for domestic students; full aid possible
ICE detention of a parent forced a student to drop classes and take a job
Federal funding cuts of $250M disrupted research across campus
key_quotes: Cornell has been subject to more than $250 million in federal funding interruptions
She's had to drop a class, pick up a job, and navigate a complicated financial aid system
If your family makes less than $65,000 a year, you pay nothing in tuition

key_points: Notoriously rigorous; no grade inflation unlike peer Ivies
Prelim exams are brutal; 78 can be below average in orgo
CS ranked top 5 nationally; STEM programs are world-class
key_quotes: I took my first orgo prelim and was below the average, I got a 78 and the average was 84
There is no reason to trash on a school that doesn't have grade inflation like the other ivies
Cornell is ranked higher in most STEM than all other Ivys

key_points: President Kotlikoff hit students with his car and fled; used official channels to mislead
University paid $30M to federal government in controversial settlement
President was appointed with no national search or faculty consultation
key_quotes: He committed at least two crimes. Hitting individuals with your car even if you're fearing for your life is illegal in NYS
Cornell has reached an agreement with the federal government...Cornell will pay $30 million
There was no job posting, no national search, no consultation with the student or faculty body

key_points: Campus is stunningly beautiful; gorges and waterfalls on doorstep
Ithaca weather is brutal in winter; warm-weather students warned repeatedly
No train station; students can feel trapped without a car
key_quotes: Just moved my daughter in...we didn't get to tour the campus prior to being accepted, but Cornell is BEAUTIFUL
Make sure she goes clothes shopping for winter wear as it is like living at Tahoe in the winter
Having a car definitely makes things A LOT more accessible. I get why some of the undergrads feel trapped up on the hill

key_points: Alumni network is exceptionally strong; Cornell grads find each other everywhere
100k starting salary considered 'not great' by some students — shows high earning expectations
Strong placement in medicine, law, engineering, and tech
key_quotes: A ton of my interviewers were Cornell alum and we were able to connect and just hit it off immediately
The job that I am interviewing for has a starting salary of 100k. Which isn't great, but I guess it's alright
Cornell is consistently one of the best universities in the world

key_points: Top STEM and professional school rankings justify cost for many
No grade inflation means your GPA is harder to earn but more credible
One student left 9 credits short and still found a $68k job — but Reddit strongly advised against it
key_quotes: I Didn't Graduate and I Came Out Fine...I got a job in my field three months ago with a starting salary of 68k
Folks, finish your degrees. Especially if you're nine credits away. Don't let OP convince you this is a good idea
Top agriculture school, top hospitality school, top engineering school, top cs school, top architecture school

key_points: High achievers who work extremely hard; intense but rewarding for the right person
Class divide is real; many students from very privileged backgrounds
Students who thrive tend to find their niche; those who don't suffer visibly
key_quotes: A lot of people here come from very privileged backgrounds, and I just don't click with people who only care about prestige
The students are either the nicest people ever, or absolute snobs
I loved the challenge. I loved studying abroad. I loved learning and being able to spread my knowledge

key_points: Leadership: president ran over students, lied publicly, faced zero criminal consequences
Mental Health: suicide risk near gorges is well-known; freshmen loneliness is endemic
Safety: underage drinking death reported; Uber driver assault reported on r/Cornell
key_quotes: He used official University mass email to try to set a skewed narrative prior to investigation
Cornell freshman dies in suspected underage drinking incident
On Sunday night I was groped by my uber driver...Uber blocked my account last night

key_points: Cornell Dairy Bar ice cream is a campus legend worth the visit
Risley Hall has a secret subbasement most students never discover
Johnson Art Museum is free, on campus, and rivals major city museums
key_quotes: The Johnson Art Museum: it's truly crazy that we have a free art museum on campus that's the size of the Everson in Syracuse
The Cornell Dairy Bar — Big Red Bear Tracks gave me more love than my ex-boyfriend ever did
After visiting for my 10 year reunion, I can confirm that the Risley subbasement is real, and you can still visit it
